# Design: Fix `.github/` Templates Generated by `setup-harness-skills`

**Date:** 2026-05-29
**Status:** Approved

## Problem

When users run `/setup-harness-skills`, two things go wrong:

1. **Issue templates (A):** Generic GitHub issue templates (bug report, feature request) are generated. They don't match the harness AC format (Story/Confidence/AC/DoD/Effort/Dependencies) enforced by `/harness-issues`.
2. **CI workflow (B):** Step 7 generates a stack-specific `.github/workflows/ci.yml` that gets the stack wrong, hardcodes commands that don't exist in the project, and overwrites existing workflows.

## Solution Overview

Three targeted changes to `output-steps.md` and one new source folder in the skill:

1. **Step 7 (CI):** Add idempotency guard; use a minimal placeholder when no workflows exist.
2. **Step 7a (new):** Write harness-format issue templates to `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`.
3. **New source files:** `github-issue-templates/harness-issue.md` and `bug-report.md` in the skill folder.

## Change 1 — Step 7: CI Workflow (idempotency + minimal skeleton)

**Guard:** Before writing, check for any `*.yml` in `.github/workflows/`. If any exist, skip Step 7 entirely and record in the setup summary:

```
⚠️ CI scaffold skipped — existing workflows found
```

**When no workflows exist:** Write a minimal stack-agnostic placeholder — no stack detection, no hardcoded test commands:

```yaml
name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      # TODO: add your build/test steps here
```

## Change 2 — Step 7a: Harness Issue Templates (new step)

Insert a new step in `output-steps.md` immediately after Step 7 with this exact heading:

```
## Step 7a — Write harness issue templates to `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/`
```

> **Note:** `output-steps.md` Step 10 is pre-existing truncated content (ends mid-sentence). Do not attempt to repair it as part of this change.

This step copies two template files from the skill's `github-issue-templates/` folder to `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` in the target project.

Idempotent: skip any file that already exists at the destination.

### Template 1: `harness-issue.md`

Covers all story types: `type:feature`, `type:task`, `type:chore`, `type:spike`. The `type:` prefix in the title distinguishes them — no separate template per type is needed.

```yaml
---
name: Harness Issue
about: Feature, task, chore, or spike — follows the harness AC template
title: '[type]: '
labels: 'status:triage'
---
## Story
As a [role], I want [capability], so that [benefit].

## Confidence: AFK ✓ / HITL ⚠
> Agent confidence: **AFK** — all sources cited below.
> (or: **HITL** — missing: [specific gap description])

## Acceptance Criteria
| # | Criterion | Type | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Given X, when Y, then Z | happy path | Spec §N.N |
| 2 | Given X, when error, then W | sad path | User statement YYYY-MM-DD |

## Definition of Done
- [ ] All AC pass (verified by agent or CI)
- [ ] PR merged to main
- [ ] No regressions in related tests
- [ ] Implementation notes written if agent deviated from spec

## Effort
Estimate: **N context window(s)**

## Dependencies
Blocked by: #NNN / None
```

### Template 2: `bug-report.md`

Bug reports have a structurally different format (steps to reproduce vs story framing), justifying a separate template. The `## Optional` section is intentionally included — it captures triage-useful context (environment, logs, workaround) that the reporter fills in at submission time and that no skill can infer programmatically.

```yaml
---
name: Bug Report
about: Something is broken — follows the harness AC template
title: 'bug: '
labels: 'status:triage,type:bug'
---
## What's broken
[Describe the observed behavior and where it occurs]

## Steps to reproduce
1.
2.
3.

## Expected behavior
[What should happen instead]

## Acceptance Criteria
| # | Criterion | Type | Source |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Given X, when Y, then Z | happy path | Reproduction steps |

## Definition of Done
- [ ] All AC pass
- [ ] PR merged to main
- [ ] No regressions

## Effort
Estimate: **N context window(s)**

## Optional
- **Environment:** [OS, browser, version, etc.]
- **Logs / screenshots:** [paste or attach]
- **Workaround known:** yes / no
```

## Change 3 — New source folder in skill

```
skills/engineering/setup-harness-skills/
  github-issue-templates/
    harness-issue.md
    bug-report.md
```

Step 7a copies both files from this folder to `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/` in the target project, following the same pattern as Step 9 (seed files to `docs/agents/`).

## Why only 2 templates

GitHub issue templates serve the *manual creation* path only. The programmatic path (`/harness-issues`) enforces the harness format at write-time and does not use these templates. For manual creation:

- All story types (feature, task, chore, spike) share the same harness structure — the `type:` title prefix is the differentiator.
- Bugs need a separate template because steps-to-reproduce replaces the story framing.
- PRDs and HITL design issues are generated by skills (`/harness-prd`, `/harness-issues`), not the GitHub "New Issue" UI.

A third template would only be justified by a structurally different format. Bug is the only type that qualifies.

## Setup Summary Lines Added

```
✅ .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/harness-issue.md written
✅ .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug-report.md written
⚠️ CI scaffold skipped — existing workflows found   (or)
✅ .github/workflows/ci.yml written (placeholder — customize for your stack)
```

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `skills/engineering/setup-harness-skills/output-steps.md` | Step 7: add idempotency guard + minimal CI; add Step 7a |
| `skills/engineering/setup-harness-skills/github-issue-templates/harness-issue.md` | New source file |
| `skills/engineering/setup-harness-skills/github-issue-templates/bug-report.md` | New source file |
